records.ety: [AS. same, adv.; akin to OS. sama, samo, adv., OHG. sam, a., sama, adv., Icel. samr, a., Sw. samme, samma, Dan. samme, Goth. sama, Russ. samuii, Gr. <?/, Skr. sama, Gr. <?/ like, L. simul at the same time, similis like, and E. some, a., -some. √191. Cf. Anomalous, Assemble, Homeopathy, Homily, Seem, v. i., Semi-, Similar, Some.]

defs: Not different or other; not another or others; identical; unchanged.
quotes: Thou art the same, and thy years shall have no end.

defs: Of like kind, species, sort, dimensions, or the like; not differing in character or in the quality or qualities compared; corresponding; not discordant; similar; like.
quotes: The ethereal vigor is in all the same.

defs: Just mentioned, or just about to be mentioned.
quotes: What ye know, the same do I know.
Do but think how well the same he spends, Who spends his blood his country to relieve.
Bees like the same odors as we do.
[He] held the same political opinions with his illustrious friend.
notes: ☞ Same is commonly preceded by the, this, or that and is often used substantively as in the citations above. In a comparative use it is followed by as or with.
